UL regular prem CI
term at least 10 years but must end before ph 65th bday
10th anniv and every subseq 10th anniv GIO of 25% increase in SA
mly prems 95% invested in range of UL funds
annual mgmt charge of 1% on each UL fund
a mly CI charge
surrender - none for first three years, fund value after that
Solvency II applies

meet the cost of medical treatment
repay a loan
meet the expenses of day to day living as probably not able to pursue work
make changes to the lifestyle
meet rehabilitation expenses

sa1_e_ans_201809_Q1_(iii):
Unit linked critical illness
benefit - fixed sa on death or earlier diagnosis
gio - on 10th anniversary and each subsequent anniv - increase sa by 25%
prems payable mly - 95% invested in range of ul funds
annual 1% management charge on the ul funds
mly - a CI risk charge 
surrender - first three years no benefit - after three years accum value of the fund


The Solvency II regulatory framework applies in the country in which the insurer writes its critical illness business.

Calculation of bel

project scr for the full run off of the business
multiply by cost of capital rate

eg select a driver which has a linear relationship

then project the values of that driver and take a fixed % of that driver

sa1_e_ans_201809_Q1_(iv):
use the cost of capital method

it is the capital held against the non hedgeable risks

it is the amount that would have to be paid to transfer the liabilities to a buyer in the market
